gnhfWorkflow.useCase: Reproduce proof and repair one failure
gnhfWorkflow.outcome: Matrix plus at most one repair commit
Preserved state plus one continuation commit

gnhfWorkflow.prompt: Iteration cap alone does not bound context/spend
gnhfWorkflow.iterations: prevent-sleep does not block Windows Update restart
gnhfWorkflow.outcome: Use --worktree and no push by default
gnhfWorkflow.useAfter: Do not inflate static proof into live proof

gnhfWorkflow.prompt: Open-ended prompts trigger archaeology and negative-proof loops
gnhfWorkflow.gitMode: No more than five active items

gnhfWorkflow.prompt: Restart may stop before commit finalization
gnhfWorkflow.tokenCap: Do not blame provider without logs

gnhfWorkflow.outcome: Up to three item commits plus report
Safe code/test commits plus runtime handoff
Recovery report plus at most one continuation commit

nightShiftRunbook.sequence: P37 → P38 → P39 → P42 as needed → P44
nightShiftRunbook.purpose: Compile and execute a finite local repair queue.
nightShiftRunbook.inputs: Clean repo; proven agent; issue sources; canonical proof
nightShiftRunbook.checkpoint: One commit per item plus queue/report update
nightShiftRunbook.stopWhen: spawn/auth/quota/network failure; 2 no-diff iterations; identical failure repeats
nightShiftRunbook.morningAction: Review P44 report, then push or open/update PR manually
nightShiftRunbook.agentRule: Use only an agent whose exact command passed P37

nightShiftRunbook.purpose: Cluster many failures by one shared root cause before repair.
nightShiftRunbook.inputs: Exact failing command; failure output; related tests
nightShiftRunbook.checkpoint: Cluster repair commit or diagnosis report commit
nightShiftRunbook.stopWhen: failures are unrelated; no evidence improvement after 2 attempts
nightShiftRunbook.morningAction: Review regression proof and remaining clusters
nightShiftRunbook.agentRule: Prefer the agent with proven repo-language/tool support

nightShiftRunbook.sequence: P37 → P38 → P41 → P42 as needed → P44
nightShiftRunbook.purpose: Repair code and fixtures without touching personal saves or accounts.
nightShiftRunbook.inputs: Safety doctrine; queue; deterministic fixtures; forbidden state paths
nightShiftRunbook.checkpoint: One commit per issue; runtime-proof handoff when needed
nightShiftRunbook.stopWhen: live proof requires personal or non-disposable state
nightShiftRunbook.morningAction: Run P08 later with disposable state only
nightShiftRunbook.agentRule: Do not choose an agent route that requires live account interaction

nightShiftRunbook.sequence: P43 → P44 or resume prior executor
nightShiftRunbook.purpose: Classify and preserve exact state before retry.
nightShiftRunbook.inputs: Exact worktree; gnhf.log; notes; launcher summary; queue/report
nightShiftRunbook.stopWhen: unknown partial mutation; missing evidence; unsafe ownership
nightShiftRunbook.morningAction: Review preserved branch and exact next command
nightShiftRunbook.agentRule: Do not rerun the same failed agent after ENOENT without new P37 proof

nightShiftRunbook.sequence: Codex native adapter failed with spawn ENOENT
nightShiftRunbook.purpose: P37 runs bounded exact-path child-process probe
nightShiftRunbook.agentRule: Choose proven adapter or configure path override

variables.meaning: repo full name or local path
what this chat must not touch
explicit test order or deferred checks
variables.example: targeted checks first; Pester and survey last

variables.meaning: known commit, PR, report, or blocker
detailed sprint prompt, plan, or file path
screenshot, transcript, log, or artifact path
finite ordered list of no more than five GNHF mutation items
variables.example: 1. schema parity; 2. stale-artifact guard; 3. technician launcher

variables.meaning: exact failing test, validator, CI check, or workflow
required checks that may not be downgraded to SKIP
variables.example: PowerShell parser; JSON schema; fixture E2E

variables.meaning: approved ignored output root for generated evidence
known or suspected reason a GNHF run stopped
variables.example: Windows update restart / provider timeout / commit failure

variables.meaning: specific .gnhf run directory or ID when resuming
exact GNHF agent specification proven by P37
base executable GNHF or ACP will spawn
external or ignored spawn-readiness evidence path
read-only issue sources used to compile the queue
variables.example: gh issue list; failing tests; TODO.md

promptSequence.useItFor: Set baseline behavior before serious repository work.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Stable operating doctrine.
promptSequence.gate: Rules are active before work.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory or bounded support scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Rules are active before work.

promptSequence.useItFor: Build or repair the complete repo-local harness and leave validated committed contributions.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The request is planning-only or another prompt owns a narrower non-harness mutation.
promptSequence.produces: Implemented harness files, validators, operator report, commit, and push or PR evidence.
promptSequence.gate: Fresh agents can route work and the exact harness validators pass.
promptSequence.authority: Full owned harness and Git/PR authority
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Static and repository validation plus commit/push evidence; runtime proof remains separate where applicable.

promptSequence.moment: Conversation Closeout + Ordered Copy-Panel Sprint Mapper
promptSequence.useItFor: A chat contains decisions, gaps, unfinished work, or several next-chat lanes.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Launch order first, ordered copy panels, then a durable sprint and factoring map.
promptSequence.gate: Context is preserved and panel display matches the recommended order.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory or bounded support scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Context preventing rework is preserved.

promptSequence.useItFor: Entering an unfamiliar, stale, or context-heavy repo.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Cited evidence pack, ranked queue, and first bounded repo mutation.
promptSequence.gate: Path-grounded evidence plus actual repo movement.
promptSequence.authority: Repo evidence + first safe bounded mutation
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Path-grounded evidence plus actual repo movement.

promptSequence.moment: Repo-Aware Ordered Sprint + Harness Factoring Distributor
promptSequence.useItFor: Known repo work needs harness, agent, application, integration ownership, and exact sequencing.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Factored, ordered, non-overlapping copy-panel sprint candidates.
promptSequence.gate: Dependencies, collisions, triggers, shared owners, and panel sequence are explicit.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory or bounded support scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Dependencies and collision risks are explicit.

promptSequence.moment: Ordered Copy-Panel Sprint Plan Pack Generator
promptSequence.useItFor: Parallel or sequential chats need one-click launch panels, explicit factoring, and a display order identical to launch order.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Launch order at the top plus one self-contained copy panel per sprint lane.
promptSequence.gate: No monolithic prompt surface, shared-preamble assembly, or duplicate ownership.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory or bounded support scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Every lane has owned and forbidden scope.

promptSequence.useItFor: The user wants cherry-picks, repairs, merges, comments, branch mutations, and PR closure.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Preserved useful work, repaired/merged/closed PRs, clean base.
promptSequence.gate: Actual Git/GitHub mutations and disposition proof.
promptSequence.then: Use P07 for remaining feature work or P12 to close.
promptSequence.authority: Full cleanup authority inside scope; preservation-first
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Actual Git/GitHub mutations and disposition proof.

promptSequence.useItFor: The sprint is known and must change code, doctrine, docs, branches, comments, PRs, contracts, or releases.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Committed or remotely mutated repo progress with validation.
promptSequence.gate: Commit or concrete Git/GitHub mutation evidence.
promptSequence.authority: Full bounded repo/Git/GitHub mutation authority
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Commit or concrete Git/GitHub mutation evidence.

promptSequence.useItFor: Static checks cannot prove the requested behavior.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Observed behavior proof or honest bounded failure.
promptSequence.gate: Observed behavior is not confused with ACK/static proof.
promptSequence.authority: Runtime mutation only inside safety boundary
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Live runtime proof

promptSequence.useItFor: Agents repeatedly waste time finding seams, symbols, contracts, or entry points.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Read-only navigation tools, catalogs, and tests.
promptSequence.gate: Navigation prerequisites and limits are honest.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory or bounded support scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Navigation prerequisites and limits are honest.

promptSequence.useItFor: Logs, evidence, secrets, or local artifacts threaten repo cleanliness.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Guardrails, hooks, validators, and cleanup guidance.
promptSequence.gate: Bad staged artifacts are blocked or warned.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory or bounded support scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Bad staged artifacts are blocked or warned.

promptSequence.useItFor: Implementation needs a repeatable PASS/SKIP/FAIL gate.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Validation matrix and machine-readable result.
promptSequence.gate: Skipped checks are not reported as passes.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory or bounded support scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Harness/static/build proof

promptSequence.useItFor: A serious sprint is complete or blocked and the next chat must continue cleanly.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Concise next-agent handoff and exact next command.
promptSequence.gate: No archaeology is required.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory or bounded support scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: No archaeology is required.

promptSequence.useItFor: A mistake, explanation, or setup problem repeats.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Small proposed rule/validator/hook changes.
promptSequence.gate: Rules remain bounded and useful.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory or bounded support scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Rules remain bounded and useful.

promptSequence.useItFor: One PR has requested changes, failing checks, drift, or scope defects.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Applied repairs, pushed commits, updated PR and review state.
promptSequence.gate: Exact-head repair and PR-update evidence.
promptSequence.authority: Existing PR branch and PR metadata
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Exact-head repair and PR-update evidence.

promptSequence.useItFor: Validated PRs or branches are ready to integrate, tag, or publish.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Merges, closures, tags, releases, refreshed canonical base.
promptSequence.gate: Merge/release artifacts and SHAs exist.
promptSequence.authority: Approved merge/release authority
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Merge/release proof

promptSequence.useItFor: A donor repo contains reusable harness, skills, schemas, or adapters.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Versioned contribution manifest/schema/adapter and repo changes.
promptSequence.gate: No duplicated or circular authority.
promptSequence.authority: Owned donor/consumer contribution surfaces
promptSequence.proofCeiling: No duplicated or circular authority.

promptSequence.useItFor: Packages, runtimes, actions, SDKs, images, or toolchains need updating.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Resolved upgrade with repaired code/config/tests/docs.
promptSequence.gate: Reproducible lockfiles and compatibility proof.
promptSequence.authority: Owned dependency and compatibility surfaces
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Reproducible lockfiles and compatibility proof.

promptSequence.useItFor: Users or technicians need executable instructions, not hidden snippets.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Repo docs, help, examples, validators, and navigation updates.
promptSequence.gate: Docs match implemented behavior and examples validate.
promptSequence.authority: Owned docs/help/examples and supporting validators
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Docs match implemented behavior and examples validate.

promptSequence.useItFor: Software, services, agents, or packages must be installed, configured, or deployed.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Applied deployment, health proof, cleanup, and rollback record.
promptSequence.gate: State/version/health evidence supports the claim.
promptSequence.then: Use P08 for live behavior or P12.
promptSequence.authority: Approved environment and deployment targets
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Deployment/health proof

promptSequence.useItFor: An opportunity row is selected and should become repo progress without rewriting another prompt.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A different prompt more directly owns the requested moment.
promptSequence.produces: Repo/PR mutations plus exact status/evidence updates for the row.
promptSequence.gate: Opportunity disposition is grounded in repo evidence.
promptSequence.authority: Selected opportunity and its owned repo scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Opportunity disposition is grounded in repo evidence.

promptSequence.useItFor: Several prompts, handoffs, or artifact requirements must become one executable mission.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: Only one already-bounded prompt is needed.
promptSequence.produces: Disposition ledger, one execution prompt, and immediate execution when safe.
promptSequence.gate: Every source requirement has a disposition and the mission is bounded.
promptSequence.authority: Source requirements + repo evidence + bounded execution authority
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Evidence-backed consolidated mission and execution proof.

promptSequence.useItFor: Several repositories could receive the same insight and verified gaps should determine the first target.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: Current circumstances rather than structural gaps should dominate the choice.
promptSequence.produces: Gap-ranked repo portfolio, exact launch order, and self-contained repo panels.
promptSequence.gate: The top repository is evidence-backed and distinguished from the largest raw gap.
promptSequence.then: Launch the top panel or use P16/P07.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory portfolio-planning authority
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Portfolio ranking proof only; no implementation proof.

promptSequence.useItFor: Current timing, access, urgency, active PRs, environment, or risk should determine which repository moves first.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The decision should be based mainly on structural gaps rather than the operating situation.
promptSequence.produces: Circumstance-ranked repo order, reassessment triggers, and self-contained repo panels.
promptSequence.gate: The ranking is timestamped, evidence-backed, and keeps gap score separate.
promptSequence.then: Launch the first panel or reassess when circumstances change.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory circumstance-planning authority
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Circumstance-ranking proof only; no implementation proof.

promptSequence.useItFor: The user has an app, screen, file, or workflow in front of them and needs practical operating instructions.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: Repository documentation must be changed or the app itself must be implemented or repaired.
promptSequence.produces: Exact first action, quick start, interface map, task recipes, safety boundaries, troubleshooting, shortcuts, and next step.
promptSequence.gate: Instructions are grounded in current evidence and uncertainty is explicit.
promptSequence.then: Use the app or use P18 to codify durable documentation.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory user-enablement authority
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Grounded guidance only; no runtime or operator-success proof.

promptSequence.useItFor: The repo should be audited to determine which user journeys deserve tutorial sprints.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The user only needs immediate coaching, or an already-selected tutorial must be implemented now.
promptSequence.produces: Ranked tutorial paths, readiness classifications, dispositions, launch order, and self-contained sprint panels.
promptSequence.gate: The top tutorial is supported by current implementation and evidence.
promptSequence.then: Launch P18, P07, or P08 according to the prerequisite.
promptSequence.authority: Advisory tutorial-portfolio planning authority
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Tutorial ranking proof only; no tutorial implementation or operator acceptance proof.

promptSequence.useItFor: The validation floor must be established before a larger autonomous lane.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A larger implementation lane should begin before its proof floor is known.
promptSequence.produces: Current matrix plus one focused repair commit when necessary.
promptSequence.gate: Proof is reproduced; required failures are repaired or explained.
promptSequence.authority: Validation floor and one focused repair commit
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Proof is reproduced; required failures are repaired or explained.

promptSequence.useItFor: A preserved run stopped because of restart, timeout, failed validation, or failed finalization.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: No preserved worktree exists or the run completed normally.
promptSequence.produces: Evidence-backed recovery and at most one continuation commit.
promptSequence.gate: No destructive cleanup; saved work is preserved and dispositioned.
promptSequence.then: Use P11/P12 after completion or P31 for commit-only repair.
promptSequence.authority: Preservation and continuation in the exact existing worktree
promptSequence.proofCeiling: No destructive cleanup; saved work is preserved and dispositioned.

promptSequence.useItFor: One concrete sprint should produce one coherent validated commit.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The work contains several independent items; use P30.
promptSequence.produces: One tracked mutation, one commit, and focused validation.
promptSequence.gate: One owned mutation is committed and validated.
promptSequence.then: Use P11/P12 or another atomic lane.
promptSequence.authority: One tracked mutation and local GNHF commit
promptSequence.proofCeiling: One owned mutation is committed and validated.

promptSequence.useItFor: The repository is unfamiliar or stale but should move in the first run.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The repo and exact mutation are already known; use P28.
promptSequence.produces: At most three candidates and the first safe mutation commit.
promptSequence.gate: First mutation occurs without repository-wide archaeology.
promptSequence.authority: Compact discovery followed by first tracked mutation
promptSequence.proofCeiling: First mutation occurs without repository-wide archaeology.

promptSequence.useItFor: A small ordered backlog is already known.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The queue is open-ended or larger than five active items.
promptSequence.produces: One coherent commit per item and final disposition ledger.
promptSequence.gate: Every supplied item is dispositioned and required proof passes.
promptSequence.then: Use P11/P12 or P27 after interruption.
promptSequence.authority: Tracked mutations limited to the supplied queue
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Every supplied item is dispositioned and required proof passes.

promptSequence.useItFor: A worktree already contains an intended change that failed validation or commit finalization.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: No intended uncommitted change exists.
promptSequence.produces: The existing change is committed or preserved with an exact blocker.
promptSequence.gate: No new feature begins; existing work reaches a known commit state.
promptSequence.authority: Existing diff repair and commit finalization only
promptSequence.proofCeiling: No new feature begins; existing work reaches a known commit state.

promptSequence.useItFor: A required test, validator, fixture, schema, or workflow is reproducibly failing.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The work is a new feature rather than a reproducible required failure.
promptSequence.produces: Root-cause repair commits and executable regression proof.
promptSequence.gate: Required failures pass without suppression or downgrade.
promptSequence.authority: Required validation, fixture, schema, test, and CI repair
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Required failures pass without suppression or downgrade.

promptSequence.useItFor: An offline harness needs deterministic parity, failure paths, or artifact integrity.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The work requires live targets or real packages.
promptSequence.produces: Bounded harness commits and final PASS/SKIP/FAIL evidence.
promptSequence.gate: Zero unexplained required failures and contained evidence.
promptSequence.then: Use P11/P12; route live proof to P08.
promptSequence.authority: Offline harness, artifact, fixture, schema, and CI mutation
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Zero unexplained required failures and contained evidence.

promptSequence.useItFor: Users should not need to recover commands from chat or guess the terminal.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The user only needs immediate coaching rather than repo mutation.
promptSequence.produces: Launcher/help/docs/example commits and validated guidance.
promptSequence.gate: One clear implemented operator path remains.
promptSequence.authority: Launcher, help, docs, examples, and operator contracts
promptSequence.proofCeiling: One clear implemented operator path remains.

promptSequence.useItFor: One evidence-backed version or toolchain update is ready for bounded execution.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The upgrade scope is broad, interactive, or unverified.
promptSequence.produces: Reproducible upgrade commits and compatibility proof.
promptSequence.gate: Only the named scope changes and checks pass.
promptSequence.authority: Named dependency manifests, lockfiles, and compatibility repairs
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Only the named scope changes and checks pass.

promptSequence.useItFor: An exact branch has review, CI, drift, regression, or scope defects.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The exact branch or target base cannot be identified.
promptSequence.produces: Scoped local repair commits and a review-ready final diff.
promptSequence.gate: Exact-head repair queue is empty and local proof passes.
promptSequence.then: Human push/review, then P15 or P12.
promptSequence.authority: Exact PR-branch local repair; no remote mutation by default
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Exact-head repair queue is empty and local proof passes.

promptSequence.useItFor: A new agent, adapter, shell domain, or workstation must be proven before a long unattended run.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The exact agent command has already passed a bounded child-process spawn test in the current shell.
promptSequence.produces: Spawnability result with exact path, timeout behavior, exit code, and next action.
promptSequence.gate: The command is spawnable without timeout and no command-presence-only claim is made.
promptSequence.then: Use P38 after PASS; fix the adapter or choose another proven agent after FAIL.
promptSequence.authority: Read-only shell and repository readiness authority
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Spawn and path proof only; no hosted inference or repository mutation proof.

promptSequence.useItFor: An issue-heavy repo needs a finite executable queue before an overnight repair run.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: A current committed queue already contains ready items with exact acceptance commands.
promptSequence.produces: Committed queue and baseline report with no more than five items.
promptSequence.gate: Every item is evidence-backed, owned, dependency-aware, and exactly testable.
promptSequence.authority: Queue and report files only; no product-code repair
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Planning artifact commit only; no repair or runtime proof.

promptSequence.useItFor: A committed queue contains ready local code repairs and the selected agent passed P37.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The work is remote-only, open-ended, lacks exact validation, or requires live interaction.
promptSequence.produces: Up to three queue-item commits plus updated queue and night report.
promptSequence.gate: Every attempted item is committed or blocked with evidence; repeated no-progress stops the run.
promptSequence.authority: Local code, tests, queue, and report within supplied scope
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Committed local repair proof only; no remote integration or live behavior claim.

promptSequence.useItFor: One exact test or validator produces many related failures that can be clustered by root cause.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: Failures are unrelated or no exact reproducible command exists.
promptSequence.produces: One repaired cluster with regression proof or a committed diagnosis report.
promptSequence.gate: At most five related failures are handled without suppressing required checks.
promptSequence.authority: One bounded root-cause cluster
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Offline failure and regression proof; no broad backlog completion claim.

promptSequence.moment: GNHF Stateful App / Game Safe Repair
promptSequence.useItFor: A stateful app or game needs code repairs but personal saves, accounts, resources, or production state must remain untouched.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: The requested proof requires unavoidable mutation of personal or non-disposable state.
promptSequence.produces: Up to three code-level repair commits and a runtime-proof handoff when needed.
promptSequence.gate: All work stays in code, tests, fixtures, or explicitly disposable runtime state.
promptSequence.then: Use P08 later for live proof; otherwise P42 or P44.
promptSequence.authority: Code, tests, deterministic fixtures, and safety report
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Code and deterministic test proof only unless disposable runtime proof is actually observed.

promptSequence.useItFor: The current queue is exhausted, stale, or blocked and a new bounded set is needed.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: Ready items remain in the current queue.
promptSequence.produces: Refreshed committed queue preserving prior dispositions.
promptSequence.gate: No product code changes and no more than five new ready items.
promptSequence.then: Return to P39, P40, or P41.
promptSequence.authority: Queue and report refresh only
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Queue freshness proof only; no repair claim.

promptSequence.useItFor: A preserved run stopped or made no progress and its exact state must be classified before retry.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: No preserved worktree or run evidence exists.
promptSequence.produces: Committed recovery report plus at most one continuation or finalization commit.
promptSequence.gate: No destructive cleanup or blind repetition of the original failure.
promptSequence.then: Resume with a proven agent or use P44.
promptSequence.authority: Exact preserved worktree and recovery artifacts
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Recovery and preserved-state proof; no claim beyond observed continuation.

promptSequence.useItFor: Overnight work must be reconciled, validated, and made reviewable.
promptSequence.doNotUseWhen: New feature implementation is still required.
promptSequence.produces: Committed closeout report, final validation matrix, clean worktree, and exact next command.
promptSequence.gate: All overnight commits and blockers are dispositioned; exit code alone is never success.
promptSequence.then: Human review, push, PR update, or next queue.
promptSequence.authority: Validation, report, and finalization of existing overnight work
promptSequence.proofCeiling: Closeout and local proof only; no push, merge, or release proof.

classLegend.whenToUse: Install operating law and repo harness
classLegend.proofGate: P00 doctrine commit and P01 harness surfaces exist
classLegend.meaning: P00 commits doctrine; P01 builds the broader repo-local harness.

classLegend.whenToUse: Preserve long-chat decisions and emit ordered launch panels
classLegend.proofGate: Context prevents rework
classLegend.meaning: Context preservation and handoff; records state without active mutation.

classLegend.whenToUse: Unknown or stale repo
classLegend.proofGate: Evidence plus first mutation
classLegend.meaning: Repository discovery plus the first safe evidence-backed action.

classLegend.whenToUse: Classify and package bounded lanes
classLegend.proofGate: Scope, order, and dependencies explicit
classLegend.meaning: Planning, sequencing, factoring, or distribution of bounded work.

classLegend.whenToUse: Clean branches, worktrees, and PRs
classLegend.proofGate: Preservation and Git/GitHub mutations
classLegend.meaning: Preservation-first cleanup of branches, worktrees, and pull requests.

classLegend.whenToUse: Any bounded repo implementation
classLegend.proofGate: Commit or mutation evidence
classLegend.meaning: Owned implementation or repository mutation with proof.

classLegend.whenToUse: Live behavior required
classLegend.proofGate: Observed behavior chain
classLegend.meaning: Live or runtime behavior must be observed and evidenced.

classLegend.whenToUse: Read-only code intelligence
classLegend.proofGate: Navigation prerequisites proven
classLegend.meaning: Read-only navigation, code intelligence, or leverage work.

classLegend.whenToUse: Artifact and secret hygiene
classLegend.proofGate: Bad staging blocked
classLegend.meaning: Safety, artifact hygiene, secret protection, and mutation guardrails.

classLegend.whenToUse: Aggregate proof
classLegend.proofGate: PASS/SKIP/FAIL matrix
classLegend.meaning: Validation, acceptance gates, and PASS/SKIP/FAIL proof.

classLegend.whenToUse: End serious sprint
classLegend.proofGate: Exact next state preserved
classLegend.meaning: Compress exact state, gaps, and next command without new implementation.

classLegend.whenToUse: Repeated pain becomes doctrine
classLegend.proofGate: Small bounded proposals
classLegend.meaning: Turn repeated friction into bounded doctrine or harness improvements.

classLegend.whenToUse: One PR is broken or reviewed
classLegend.proofGate: Exact-head repair and PR update
classLegend.meaning: Review and repair a specific pull request at its exact head.

classLegend.whenToUse: Ready work must land
classLegend.proofGate: Merge/tag/release evidence
classLegend.meaning: Integrate, merge, tag, release, or refresh the canonical base.

classLegend.whenToUse: Portable insight spans repos
classLegend.proofGate: Versioned ownership-safe contribution
classLegend.meaning: Contribute portable insight or interoperability across repositories.

classLegend.whenToUse: Upgrade packages/toolchain
classLegend.proofGate: Reproducible compatibility proof
classLegend.meaning: Upgrade dependencies or toolchains with reproducible compatibility proof.

classLegend.whenToUse: Create durable docs or learn the current app
classLegend.proofGate: Docs validate; guidance is evidence-grounded
classLegend.meaning: Documentation, tutorials, and in-context app guidance tied to real behavior.

classLegend.whenToUse: Install/deploy/upgrade target
classLegend.proofGate: State/version/health proof
classLegend.meaning: Install, deploy, upgrade, or operate a target with health proof.

classLegend.whenToUse: Selected opportunity must become work
classLegend.proofGate: Repo disposition plus row updates
classLegend.meaning: Execute a selected opportunity and update its disposition.

classLegend.whenToUse: Many prompts become one bounded mission
classLegend.proofGate: Disposition ledger plus execution evidence
classLegend.meaning: Consolidate several prompts without losing requirements; execute the immediate mission when safe.

classLegend.whenToUse: Choose which repo receives an insight first by verified gaps
classLegend.proofGate: Evidence-backed portfolio ranking
classLegend.meaning: Prioritize repositories by actionable gap, leverage, tractability, and proof readiness.

classLegend.whenToUse: Choose which repo moves first under current conditions
classLegend.proofGate: Timestamped circumstance ranking
classLegend.meaning: Prioritize repositories by urgency, access, timing, dependencies, risk, and available environment.

classLegend.whenToUse: Rank user journeys worth tutorial investment
classLegend.proofGate: Evidence-backed tutorial ranking and readiness dispositions
classLegend.meaning: Plan tutorial work only when the user journey is implemented, teachable, safe, and independently verifiable.

classLegend.whenToUse: The validation floor must be established before a larger autonomous lane.
classLegend.proofGate: Proof is reproduced; required failures are repaired or explained.
classLegend.fillRole: Reproduce the canonical proof floor and repair at most one required failure
classLegend.meaning: Current matrix plus one focused repair commit when necessary.

classLegend.whenToUse: A preserved run stopped because of restart, timeout, failed validation, or failed finalization.
classLegend.proofGate: No destructive cleanup; saved work is preserved and dispositioned.
classLegend.fillRole: Preserve and continue an interrupted GNHF worktree
classLegend.meaning: Evidence-backed recovery and at most one continuation commit.

classLegend.whenToUse: One concrete sprint should produce one coherent validated commit.
classLegend.proofGate: One owned mutation is committed and validated.
classLegend.meaning: One tracked mutation, one commit, and focused validation.

classLegend.whenToUse: The repository is unfamiliar or stale but should move in the first run.
classLegend.proofGate: First mutation occurs without repository-wide archaeology.
classLegend.fillRole: Recover compact repo truth and make the first safe mutation
classLegend.meaning: At most three candidates and the first safe mutation commit.

classLegend.whenToUse: A small ordered backlog is already known.
classLegend.proofGate: Every supplied item is dispositioned and required proof passes.
classLegend.fillRole: Execute a supplied queue of one to five items
classLegend.meaning: One coherent commit per item and final disposition ledger.

classLegend.whenToUse: A worktree already contains an intended change that failed validation or commit finalization.
classLegend.proofGate: No new feature begins; existing work reaches a known commit state.
classLegend.fillRole: Repair and commit an existing unfinished mutation
classLegend.meaning: The existing change is committed or preserved with an exact blocker.

classLegend.whenToUse: A required test, validator, fixture, schema, or workflow is reproducibly failing.
classLegend.proofGate: Required failures pass without suppression or downgrade.
classLegend.fillRole: Repair a bounded failing validation or CI lane
classLegend.meaning: Root-cause repair commits and executable regression proof.

classLegend.whenToUse: An offline harness needs deterministic parity, failure paths, or artifact integrity.
classLegend.proofGate: Zero unexplained required failures and contained evidence.
classLegend.fillRole: Harden offline contracts, fixtures, repeatability, and artifacts
classLegend.meaning: Bounded harness commits and final PASS/SKIP/FAIL evidence.

classLegend.whenToUse: Users should not need to recover commands from chat or guess the terminal.
classLegend.proofGate: One clear implemented operator path remains.
classLegend.fillRole: Build one clear technician or operator workflow
classLegend.meaning: Launcher/help/docs/example commits and validated guidance.

classLegend.whenToUse: One evidence-backed version or toolchain update is ready for bounded execution.
classLegend.proofGate: Only the named scope changes and checks pass.
classLegend.fillRole: Upgrade one named dependency or toolchain scope
classLegend.meaning: Reproducible upgrade commits and compatibility proof.

classLegend.whenToUse: An exact branch has review, CI, drift, regression, or scope defects.
classLegend.proofGate: Exact-head repair queue is empty and local proof passes.
classLegend.fillRole: Repair one exact PR branch locally
classLegend.meaning: Scoped local repair commits and a review-ready final diff.

classLegend.whenToUse: Before a new unattended agent or adapter is trusted.
classLegend.proofGate: Exact child-process spawn succeeds inside a bounded timeout.
classLegend.meaning: Prove real executable resolution before GNHF opens; avoids ENOENT and command-presence false positives.

classLegend.whenToUse: An issue-heavy repo needs a finite committed queue before repair.
classLegend.proofGate: Every item has evidence, owned paths, dependencies, validation, and a state.
classLegend.meaning: Commit the executable queue so the later night run is finite and GNHF has a local deliverable.

classLegend.whenToUse: A committed queue contains ready local repairs.
classLegend.proofGate: Up to three items are committed or blocked; repeated no-progress stops.
classLegend.meaning: Primary repeatable overnight executor with one commit per queue item and checkpoint report updates.

classLegend.whenToUse: Many failures share a reproducible root cause.
classLegend.proofGate: One bounded cluster passes or a diagnosis report is committed.
classLegend.meaning: Useful for AxTask-style noisy test suites; cluster before repairing rather than chasing unrelated failures.

classLegend.whenToUse: A game or stateful app requires code repair without personal-state mutation.
classLegend.proofGate: No saves, accounts, live resources, or production state are touched.
classLegend.meaning: Useful for BlacksmithGuild-style work where code and fixture proof must precede disposable runtime proof.

classLegend.whenToUse: The committed queue is exhausted, stale, or blocked.
classLegend.proofGate: Prior dispositions are preserved and no more than five new items are added.
classLegend.meaning: Refill the night queue without mixing planning and implementation.

classLegend.whenToUse: A run stopped, failed to spawn, or produced no useful diff.
classLegend.proofGate: Failure is classified and preserved before at most one continuation commit.
classLegend.meaning: Handles ENOENT, quota, auth, timeout, no-diff, validation, and finalization failures without destructive reruns.

classLegend.whenToUse: Overnight work needs final proof and handoff.
classLegend.proofGate: Existing work is validated, reported, committed, and clean.
classLegend.meaning: Closes the night without starting another feature or mistaking process exit for delivery.
